charcoal-canvas.$description: Charcoal Canvas — Page background, the dominant surface across all sections and the base layer behind full-bleed photography

bone.$description: Bone — All body text, meta labels, navigation dots, and the dominant hairline border color — serves as the single foreground tone in this near-monochrome system

graphite.$description: Graphite — Secondary icon strokes, subtle list dividers, and muted fill details — sits one step darker than the canvas for low-contrast decoration

ash.$description: Ash — Inactive or resting state for navigation dots and quiet UI affordances

neue-haas-unica.$description: The sole text family for all UI copy: meta labels at 16px, body at 18px, list/links at 24px, subheadings at 27px, section headings at 36px. Weight 400 is default; 700 is reserved for emphasis in the meta block and active labels. The 1.10 lineHeight on 36px headings creates tight, architectural vertical rhythm.

display-custom-or-system-fallback.$description: Reserved exclusively for the two program words ('IMAGINE', 'PROGRAM') that span the full viewport width and bleed past both edges. This face is wide, heavy, with rounded terminals — it is the visual signature of the entire site and carries the brand's identity more than any other element. When recreating, use a heavy extended grotesque (e.g. Obviously Wide Black, Neue Machina Black) at a size that forces letter cropping at the viewport edges.

charcoal-canvas.$description: Surface level 1: The base layer — page background and the surface visible between photographic panels

photographic-surface.$description: Surface level 2: Full-bleed image/video panels that replace the canvas at section boundaries. Same hex but visually distinct due to content.

text-surface.$description: Surface level 3: No actual elevated surface — text sits directly on the canvas or photography. The 'elevation' is purely typographic scale.
